phases.title: Mobile push-receipt observability + on-device verification runbook
phases.notes: Code/docs deliverables depend on nothing; the manual MA-1 step exercises Phase 1 backend and must run after Phase 1.

requirements.statement: UrgencyLevel gains a field channel: str = "both".
A field_validator on channel raises ValueError for any value not in {sms, push, both}.
Configs omitting channel still load; channel is not required on critical/low.
_determine_action returns the matched SMS-tier level's channel (sms|push|both) for the 5-8 tiers.
Critical level returns phone_call (corroborated) else sms fallback; never push/both.
Scores below the lowest SMS tier return log_only.
Level matching remains order-independent (sort by min_score desc).
process_event sends push via _maybe_send_push when resolved action is push or both.
Resolved action sms/both sends Twilio SMS via _execute_sms, suppressed when _user_already_notified; the push half is not gated by this SMS suppression (bounded only by 1.3c dedup).
phone_call action places the call AND sends an additive push via _maybe_send_push (AD-2); confirmation/stop SMS unchanged.
A second cycle for the same event does not re-push (existing _maybe_send_push dedup on a prior push record preserved).
log_only sends neither SMS nor push.

Acknowledged-event update branch sends update SMS AND an additive push via _maybe_send_push(is_update=True) (AD-3); is_update dedup-bypass intended so each update pushes; is_update parameter retained.
With push disabled/empty tokens, a both/push tier sends no push and both still sends SMS (behavior-preserving default).
config.yaml and config.example.yaml set channel: both on high and medium; example keeps its push block disabled; config.yaml stays without a push block (default disabled).
The critical/phone_call call placement, confirmation/stop SMS, retry loop, cooldown, and acknowledgment are unchanged; the only addition to that flow is the additive push (1.3b).

Stale 'additive / never replaces' wording in push_client.py (class/send_push docstrings), _maybe_send_push docstring, and the process_event dispatch comment is corrected to the channel model; 'never replaces' no longer appears under sentinel/.
config-reference.md documents the channel field: values sms/push/both, default both, applies to high/medium, ignored for critical/log_only.
No file under docs/ or sentinel/, nor CLAUDE.md, retains the claim that push 'never replaces' the Twilio channels.
mobile-app.md, pipeline.md, architecture.md describe routing 5-8 by channel and that 9-10 sends call+SMS plus an additive push (AD-2) and acknowledged updates send SMS plus an additive push (AD-3).
Docs state the shipped default (channel both, push disabled) is SMS-only and that switching to push removes the Twilio SMS cost.
CLAUDE.md no longer calls push 'additive Expo push'; reflects the per-tier channel.
.claude/rules/corroboration.md notes _determine_action now resolves channel from each SMS-tier level's channel setting.
mobile/package.json defines a typecheck script running tsc --noEmit.
All mobile TypeScript type-checks cleanly under strict mode (tsc --noEmit exits 0).
usePushReceiver registers received + response listeners (v54.0.0 API), logs payload, removes listeners on cleanup. No automated behavioral oracle (no JS test runner); verified by review + MA-1.
PushPanel displays the most recently received push title+body (or placeholder). No automated behavioral oracle; verified by review + MA-1.
mobile-push-setup.md documents the full manual e2e: eas projectId, dev build, permission, copy token, paste+enable, set channel, --test-alert push, confirm receipt.
Runbook states steps 1-2 are manual interactive (eas/Apple) and that the projectId placeholder is replaced by eas, not hardcoded.
Mobile changes do not alter registerForPush.ts token minting or the copy flow (additive only). Verified by review/diff and MA-1 (a broken mint fails MA-1).

tests.name: test_channel_field_defaults_to_both
tests.assertion: UrgencyLevel(min_score=7, action='sms').channel == 'both'

tests.name: test_channel_accepts_each_valid_value
tests.assertion: channel in {sms,push,both} constructs and round-trips

tests.name: test_channel_rejects_invalid_value
tests.assertion: channel='email' raises ValidationError mentioning channel

tests.name: test_config_loads_without_channel_keys
tests.assertion: config without channel loads; high/medium default to 'both'

tests.name: test_determine_action_high_returns_channel
tests.assertion: score 7 returns high.channel (push/both/sms as configured)

tests.name: test_determine_action_medium_returns_channel
tests.assertion: score 5 returns medium.channel (medium level injected per fixture note)

tests.name: test_determine_action_critical_call_when_corroborated
tests.assertion: score 10, sources 2, corr 1 -> 'phone_call'

tests.name: test_determine_action_critical_single_source_fallback_sms
tests.assertion: score 10, sources 1, corr 2 -> 'sms' (never push/both)

tests.name: test_determine_action_low_logs_only
tests.assertion: score 3 -> 'log_only' (low level injected per fixture note)

tests.name: test_channel_push_sends_push_not_sms
tests.assertion: high.channel=push, push enabled, score7: send_push called once, send_sms not called; one push record, no sms record

tests.name: test_channel_both_sends_push_and_sms
tests.assertion: high.channel=both, push enabled, score7: send_push once AND send_sms once; one push + one sms record

tests.name: test_channel_sms_sends_sms_not_push
tests.assertion: high.channel=sms, push enabled, score7: send_sms called, send_push not called

tests.name: test_critical_event_sends_additive_push
tests.assertion: push enabled+token, score10 sources2: call flow runs AND send_push called once; one push record exists

tests.name: test_push_self_dedup_on_second_cycle
tests.assertion: high.channel=push, push enabled, process_event twice same event: send_push called exactly once

tests.name: test_acknowledged_update_sends_sms_and_push
tests.assertion: push enabled, acknowledged event updated: _send_update_sms fires AND send_push called (is_update bypass)

tests.name: test_push_update_bypasses_dedup
tests.assertion: EXISTING test must keep passing: _maybe_send_push(..., is_update=True) still calls send_push once (is_update param retained)

tests.name: test_default_both_with_push_disabled_sends_sms_only
tests.assertion: push disabled, high.channel=both, score7: send_sms once, send_push not called, no push record

tests.name: test_critical_flow_unchanged_regression
tests.assertion: existing critical-call lifecycle tests pass under new code

tests.name: gate_no_never_replaces_in_code
tests.assertion: phrase 'never replaces' absent under sentinel/ after docstring/comment fixes

tests.name: doc_channel_field_documented
tests.assertion: config-reference documents the channel field and the both value as code spans (neither pre-exists)

tests.name: doc_no_never_replaces
tests.assertion: phrase 'never replaces' absent from docs/, sentinel/, CLAUDE.md (currently in config-reference.md, pipeline.md, push_client.py)

tests.name: doc_explanations_reference_channel
tests.gate_command: grep -qF '`channel`' docs/explanation/mobile-app.md && grep -qF '`channel`' docs/explanation/pipeline.md && grep -qF '`channel`' docs/explanation/architecture.md
tests.assertion: each of the three explanation docs references the backticked channel field (none does today)

tests.name: doc_claudemd_updated
tests.assertion: CLAUDE.md no longer says 'additive Expo' (today line 4: 'optional additive Expo **push**')

tests.name: gate_typecheck_script
tests.assertion: package.json defines a typecheck script (absent today)

tests.name: gate_typecheck_passes
tests.assertion: tsc --noEmit exits 0 under strict mode (type-correctness only; does not verify listener/panel behavior)

tests.name: gate_runbook_exists
tests.assertion: runbook file exists

tests.name: gate_runbook_covers_provisioning
tests.assertion: runbook covers eas provisioning + the test command

tests.name: MA-1
tests.assertion: MANUAL, NON-GATING: on the physical iPhone, ./run.sh --test-alert push shows a notification that appears in PushPanel; requires real projectId + dev build + Phase 1 backend

1: .venv/bin/python -c "from sentinel.config import UrgencyLevel; assert UrgencyLevel(min_score=7, action='sms').channel == 'both'"
.venv/bin/ruff check sentinel/config.py sentinel/alerts/state_machine.py sentinel/alerts/push_client.py tests/test_config.py tests/test_state_machine.py
